section.heading: Charge of property transferred to the Federal Government

subsection.heading: In General.—
subsection.chapeau: Except as provided in subsection (b), the Administrator of General Services shall have charge of—

paragraph.content: all land and other property which has been or may be assigned, set off, or conveyed to the Federal Government in payment of debts;
all trusts created for the use of the Government in payment of debts due the Government; and

paragraph.chapeau: the sale and disposal of land—

subparagraph.content: assigned or set off to the Government in payment of debt; or
vested in the Government by mortgage or other security for the payment of debts.

subsection.heading: Nonapplication.—
subsection.chapeau: This section does not apply to—

paragraph.content: real estate which has been or shall be assigned, set off, or conveyed to the Government in payment of debts arising under the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 (26 U.S.C. 1 et seq.); or
trusts created for the use of the Government in payment of debts arising under the Code and due the Government.

html:tr.style: font-size:6pt; border-bottom:1px solid black; border-top:1px solid black; -uslm-lc:h1;

html:th.style: min-width: 44.0pt; text-align:center; vertical-align:middle; border-right:1px solid black;
width:68.0pt ; max-width:68.0pt; text-align:center; vertical-align:middle; border-right:1px solid black; border-left:1px solid black;
width:94.0pt ; max-width:94.0pt; text-align:center; vertical-align:middle; border-left:1px solid black;

html:td.style: text-align:left; vertical-align:top; border-right:1px solid black; padding-right:2pt;
text-align:left; vertical-align:top; border-right:1px solid black; border-left:1px solid black; padding-right:2pt; padding-left: 2pt;
text-align:left; vertical-align:top; border-left:1px solid black; padding-left: 2pt;

note.p: In subsection (a), the words “Except as provided in subsection (b)” are added for clarity.

In subsection (b)(1), the words “the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 (26 U.S.C. 1 et seq.)” are substituted for “the internal-revenue laws” for clarity and for consistency in the revised title and with other titles of the United States Code.

note.heading: References in Text
note.p: The Internal Revenue Code of 1986, referred to in subsec. (b)(1), is classified to Title 26, Internal Revenue Code.
